headline: Russia-Ukraine Ceasefire Hits Final Day With 200+ Clashes Logged; Warsh Senate Vote Sets Up Powell Successor; Markets Tee Up CPI Tuesday at Fresh ATH
summary: Day 3 — the final day — of Trump's May 9–11 Russia-Ukraine ceasefire arrived with no announced extension and Reuters reporting 200+ battlefield clashes and three civilians killed by Russian drones over the weekend, even as both sides remained nominally inside the framework. The Senate is scheduled to vote on Kevin Warsh's nomination as Federal Reserve Chair this week, with Republicans' 53-seat majority and a confirmed Fetterman crossover making confirmation the base case ahead of Powell's May 15 exit. The fourth round of US-Iran indirect talks is being held in Oman with Iran still reviewing the 14-point MOU; Trump-Xi summit preparations enter their final 72 hours as USAF C-17 transports stage in Beijing and Boeing's Kelly Ortberg and Citigroup's CEO confirm they will fly with the president. Markets sit at all-time highs heading into Tuesday's CPI print (consensus +3.8% headline, +2.7% core).

highlights.title: Russia-Ukraine Ceasefire Day 3: 200+ Battlefield Clashes, 3 Civilians Killed, No Extension Announced
highlights.summary: The final day of the Trump-brokered May 9–11 Russia-Ukraine truce opens with Reuters and Ukrainian officials reporting more than 200 battlefield engagements since Saturday and three civilians killed in Russian drone strikes near the frontline overnight. Ukraine has not formally declared Russia in violation; Moscow says it 'responded in kind' to dozens of Ukrainian drones. The Kremlin has stressed the ceasefire is 'for three days, not longer,' deflating prior speculation about an extension. The 1,000-prisoner swap component of the deal is reported to have proceeded. Trump's framing — 'beginning of the end of a very long, deadly, and hard fought War' — now faces its first test: whether anything continues after midnight.

highlights.title: Warsh Senate Floor Vote This Week — Fetterman Crosses Over, Confirmation the Base Case; Powell Exits May 15
highlights.summary: Kevin Warsh's full Senate confirmation as Federal Reserve Chair is queued for this week. The April Banking Committee vote was the first fully partisan committee vote in the Fed's history (13-11 along party lines), but Republicans hold a 53-seat floor majority and Sen. John Fetterman (D-PA) has publicly confirmed he will vote yes. Powell's term as Chair expires May 15; if Warsh is confirmed in time, the transition will be unusually compressed. Former Fed economist Claudia Sahm called the partisan posture 'not normal,' but markets are pricing confirmation as near-certain. Warsh's balance-sheet reduction views could imply a more aggressive QT path than the rate dot plot alone suggests.

highlights.title: Trump-Xi Summit T-3 Days: C-17s Already in Beijing, Boeing and Citigroup CEOs Fly Along; CFR Calls It 'China's Upper Hand'
highlights.summary: Trump's Beijing state visit (May 14–15) is 72 hours away. Four USAF C-17 transports have landed at Beijing Capital with the presidential motorcade and security gear; Boeing CEO Kelly Ortberg and Citigroup's CEO will accompany the president. Fortune's Steve Hanke and CFR both argue that 'China has the upper hand' — six years of preparing rare-earth leverage, Strait of Hormuz exposure for US oil, and a Taiwan agenda Xi has reportedly elevated to the top of the talks. The Board of Trade framework remains the expected headline deliverable, with potential easing of 14nm/7nm chip equipment controls and Chinese soybean/Boeing commitments under discussion. Iran war remains the unresolved precondition Beijing has flagged.

perspectives.title: Day 3 of the Ceasefire: Diplomatic Foundation or Cosmetic Pause?
perspectives.subtitle: Trump frames the final day as the start of something; Reuters and Kyiv document 200+ engagements; the Kremlin pre-empts any extension talk

sources.name: Trump / Truth Social
sources.quote: Hopefully it is the beginning of the end of a very long, deadly, and hard fought War. Talks are continuing.

sources.name: Reuters / Ukrainian Officials
sources.quote: Three people were killed in Russian drone strikes near the frontline, and more than 200 battlefield clashes have broken out since early Saturday — even as the ceasefire technically remains in force.

sources.name: Kremlin Briefing
sources.quote: The ceasefire is for three days, not longer.

perspectives.title: Trump-Xi Summit: Negotiating From Strength or Walking Into a Trap?
perspectives.subtitle: White House aides cast the trip as deal-making leverage; CFR and Brookings warn China spent six years stockpiling counter-leverage

sources.name: Trump / White House framing (via CNBC)
sources.quote: Boeing's Kelly Ortberg and Citigroup's CEO will join Trump on the China visit — a signal that real commercial deals are on the table, not just diplomatic theater.

sources.name: Steve Hanke / Fortune
sources.quote: Trump thinks he's flying to Beijing with leverage. China spent six years making sure he doesn't have any — through rare-earth dominance, Hormuz pressure on US oil, and a Taiwan agenda Xi is now placing at the top.

quotes.text: Talks are continuing on ending the war. Hopefully, it is the beginning of the end of a very long, deadly, and hard fought War.
The transition of leadership at the Federal Reserve will, as in every prior instance, proceed in accordance with the institution's tradition of independence from partisan dynamics.
quotes.source: Federal Reserve, prepared remarks ahead of May 15 exit

map_hotspots.title: Ukraine Front — Ceasefire Day 3 (Final): 200+ Clashes Logged Weekend
map_hotspots.summary: Day 3 of the May 9–11 Trump-brokered Russia-Ukraine ceasefire ends today. Reuters reports 200+ battlefield clashes since Saturday and three civilians killed in Russian drone strikes near the frontline. Both sides remain formally in the framework; the Kremlin says the truce is 'for three days, not longer.' No extension announced as of midday.

map_hotspots.title: Moscow — Kremlin Caps Truce at 3 Days, Rejects Extension Speculation
map_hotspots.summary: Kremlin spokesperson reiterated the May 9–11 truce is bounded; reciprocal drone interceptions continued overnight. No formal joint statement scheduled for end-of-truce.

map_hotspots.title: Muscat — Fourth Round of US-Iran Indirect MOU Talks Today
map_hotspots.summary: Iranian and US delegations meet via Omani mediation for round four of indirect MOU negotiations. Iran's MFA spokesperson Baghaei: Tehran is 'still reviewing' the 14-point US proposal; key sticking points include duration of enrichment moratorium, Strait of Hormuz transit, and the Rainbow Site tritium-facility allegations.

map_hotspots.title: Beijing — Trump-Xi Summit T-3 Days: USAF C-17 Transports Staged
map_hotspots.summary: Four USAF C-17 transports have landed at Beijing Capital with the presidential motorcade and security gear ahead of the May 14–15 state visit. Boeing CEO Kelly Ortberg and Citigroup's CEO confirmed to accompany Trump. Taiwan, semiconductor export controls, rare earths, and the Iran war are all in the agenda. China is reported to have prepared rare-earth and Hormuz-linked oil leverage.

map_hotspots.title: Strait of Hormuz — US-Iran Trade Fire May 7; Trump Calls Strikes 'A Love Tap'
map_hotspots.summary: Last week's exchange in the Strait — three US warships fired on per CENTCOM, US strikes on two Iranian-flagged ships per WaPo — has not formally ended the April 8 ceasefire. Gas prices topped $4.55/gallon, the highest since the war began. Strait flow remains constrained, capping UAE oil-exit upside.

map_hotspots.title: Wellington — A-League Final Berth Secured 2-0 vs Brisbane Roar
map_hotspots.summary: Wellington Phoenix beat Brisbane Roar 2-0 in extra time to book a historic A-League grand final spot — a rare domestic positive for NZ sports headlines on a heavy international news day.

map_hotspots.title: Tenerife — MV Hondius Hantavirus Cruise Disembarks; One Kiwi Aboard
map_hotspots.summary: MV Hondius docked Port of Granadilla overnight following WHO confirmation of Andes-virus hantavirus infections (3 deaths). Single confirmed NZ passenger may face 42-day quarantine on return per WHO guidance. Stuff and 1News tracking.

map_hotspots.title: Washington — Warsh Senate Vote Pending, CPI Tuesday, Fed Transition May 15
map_hotspots.summary: Senate floor vote on Kevin Warsh as Fed Chair is expected this week. Fetterman (D-PA) confirmed yes, Republicans hold 53 seats — confirmation is the base case. April CPI prints Tuesday (consensus +3.8% headline, +2.7% core); Powell's term ends May 15.

event_updates.title: Russia-Ukraine Ceasefire Negotiations
event_updates.latest_summary: Day 3 (final) of Trump's May 9–11 ceasefire arrives with 200+ battlefield clashes logged over the weekend and three civilians killed in Russian drone strikes per Reuters. The Kremlin has explicitly capped the truce at three days; no extension announced. The 1,000-prisoner swap is reported to have proceeded. The next 24 hours determine whether the framework becomes a foundation for further talks or collapses into renewed combat operations.

event_updates.title: US-Iran War Ceasefire Negotiations
event_updates.latest_summary: The fourth round of indirect US-Iran MOU talks is being held in Oman today (May 11), ahead of Trump's Middle East travel later this week. Iran's MFA spokesperson Baghaei said Tehran is still 'reviewing' the 14-point US proposal. Sticking points: duration of enrichment moratorium, Hormuz transit (Iran wants resolved first), the unaddressed Rainbow Site tritium-facility allegations. The Strait of Hormuz fire exchange of May 7 has not formally ended the April 8 ceasefire; Trump called the strikes 'just a love tap.'

event_updates.title: US-China Trade & Chip Export Controls
event_updates.latest_summary: Trump-Xi Beijing summit is 3 days away (May 14–15). Four USAF C-17 transports already landed at Beijing Capital; Boeing CEO Kelly Ortberg and Citigroup's CEO will accompany Trump. Expected deliverables: Board of Trade framework, Chinese soybean/Boeing purchase commitments, possible easing of 14nm/7nm chip-equipment export controls. CFR, Brookings, and Fortune's Steve Hanke argue China holds the structural upper hand through rare-earth leverage, Hormuz-linked oil exposure, and an elevated Taiwan agenda. The Iran war remains the precondition Beijing has flagged but Trump is proceeding regardless.
